notes: FIA REF_SPECIES confirms SPCD 832 is chestnut oak with symbol QUPR2. TNRS/WFO returned Quercus michauxii, but that is swamp chestnut oak. USFS FEIS notes Quercus prinus was split into Q. michauxii and Q. montana; POWO accepts Q. montana. Use Q. montana for FIA chestnut oak.

bien_candidate_checked: Callitropsis nootkatensis; Cupressus nootkatensis; Xanthocyparis nootkatensis
notes: TNRS/WFO accepted name is Callitropsis nootkatensis. USDA PLANTS uses Callitropsis nootkatensis for Alaska cedar. BIEN reports ranges for Callitropsis nootkatensis and Cupressus nootkatensis, but not Xanthocyparis nootkatensis. Use TNRS/USDA-aligned Callitropsis nootkatensis.

bien_candidate_checked: Nyssa biflora; Nyssa sylvatica; Nyssa aquatica; Nyssa sylvatica var. biflora
notes: TNRS accepts Nyssa biflora, but BIEN has no range for Nyssa biflora or Nyssa sylvatica var. biflora. BIEN has ranges for Nyssa sylvatica and N. aquatica, but those are broader/different taxa; using them would blur swamp tupelo ecology. Leave out of main analysis and document unless an alternate range source is added later.

TNRS accepts Metrosideros polymorpha and the name/common name match ohia lehua. BIEN reports no range. This Hawaii endemic/foundation tree would need an alternate range source; leave out of main analysis and document because it is nonessential to the current national CWM analysis.

bien_candidate_checked: Acer leucoderme; Acer saccharum subsp. leucoderme; Acer saccharum
notes: TNRS maps Acer leucoderme to Acer saccharum subsp. leucoderme; BIEN has no range for either Acer leucoderme or the subspecies, but does have Acer saccharum. Do not use broad Acer saccharum because sugar maple has a much broader/cooler range than chalk maple. Leave out of main analysis and document because CWM weight is low.
